transactions.fraudReason: Unusually large deposit inconsistent with account history
transactions.fraudExplanation: Account averages GHS 200 deposits. This GHS 15,000 deposit exceeds the rolling 30-day average by 7,400%. Source of funds unverified. New device used at a different agent location.
transactions.recommendedAction: Place 48-hour hold on funds and request source-of-funds documentation

transactions.blockReason: Attempted rapid cash-out of suspicious deposit. Funds currently under 48-hour hold.
transactions.fraudReason: Rapid cash-out attempt following suspicious large deposit
transactions.fraudExplanation: Withdrawal of GHS 14,500 attempted only 26 minutes after a flagged GHS 15,000 deposit. Classic deposit-and-flee pattern. Different agent location from the deposit.
transactions.recommendedAction: Block transaction and escalate to fraud investigation team

transactions.fraudReason: Repeated near-identical transfer amounts within short window
transactions.fraudExplanation: Second transfer of ~GHS 480 within 27 minutes. Amounts appear structured to stay below the GHS 500 auto-review threshold.
transactions.recommendedAction: Flag for monitoring; block if third transfer occurs within 1 hour

transactions.fraudReason: Structuring pattern detected — third small transfer in rapid succession
transactions.fraudExplanation: Three transfers totalling GHS 1,350 sent to different recipients within 65 minutes. All amounts cluster near GHS 450, suggesting deliberate structuring to evade the GHS 500 monitoring threshold.
transactions.recommendedAction: Escalate to fraud case review; temporarily hold remaining wallet balance

transactions.blockReason: Structuring pattern confirmed. Fourth rapid small transfer blocked by AI fraud engine.
transactions.fraudExplanation: Fourth transfer attempt of GHS 490 blocked automatically. Combined total of GHS 1,840 across 4 transfers in under 2 hours, all below the GHS 500 threshold, sent to distinct recipients. Pattern is consistent with smurfing or layering activity.
transactions.recommendedAction: Block all outgoing transactions and freeze wallet pending investigation

transactions.fraudReason: Unusually high transaction amount; Transaction at unusual hour
transactions.fraudExplanation: GHS 8,500 deposit at 02:15 AM is highly irregular. Deposits above GHS 5,000 at this hour have a 94% correlation with synthetic deposit fraud in Ghanaian MoMo data. No prior deposit history for this account.
transactions.recommendedAction: Hold funds for 24 hours, request source-of-funds documentation, verify with agent

transactions.blockReason: Blocked: high-risk transfer_out (risk score 95) linked to suspicious deposit at unusual hour
transactions.fraudReason: Outgoing transaction follows suspicious deposit; Transaction at unusual hour
transactions.fraudExplanation: Transfer of GHS 8,000 attempted 23 minutes after flagged GHS 8,500 deposit, both at 2 AM. Near-total drain of deposited funds. Classic synthetic-deposit-and-drain pattern executed during off-hours to evade monitoring.
transactions.recommendedAction: Block transaction and escalate to fraud investigation team

transactions.blockReason: Blocked: second outgoing attempt after suspicious deposit. Wallet frozen pending investigation.
transactions.fraudReason: Outgoing transaction follows suspicious deposit; Multiple outgoing attempts post-suspicious-deposit
transactions.fraudExplanation: Second outgoing transaction attempt within 50 minutes of suspicious deposit. Wallet already frozen from prior blocked transfer. Cashout at different location suggests coordinated drain attempt.
transactions.recommendedAction: Keep wallet frozen, escalate entire transaction chain to compliance team

fraudAlerts.alertMessage: GHS 15,000 deposited into account 0207654321 (Ama Boateng) at Kumasi - Kejetia Market. This amount is 7,400% above the account's 30-day deposit average of GHS 200. A new device and unfamiliar agent were used.
fraudAlerts.recommendedAction: Place 48-hour hold on funds. Request proof of source of funds from account holder before releasing.

fraudAlerts.alertMessage: Account 0207654321 (Ama Boateng) attempted to withdraw GHS 14,500 only 26 minutes after a flagged GHS 15,000 deposit. Transaction was automatically blocked. This matches the deposit-and-flee fraud pattern.
fraudAlerts.recommendedAction: Keep wallet frozen. Escalate case to fraud investigation team for in-person verification of account holder identity.

fraudAlerts.alertMessage: Account 0271112233 (Kofi Asante) has sent 3 transfers totalling GHS 1,350 within 65 minutes from Takoradi. Each amount (GHS 450, 480, 420) is below the GHS 500 auto-review threshold, indicating potential structuring.
fraudAlerts.recommendedAction: Monitor for additional small transfers. If a fourth transfer occurs within the next hour, block outgoing transactions automatically.

fraudAlerts.alertMessage: Fourth rapid transfer of GHS 490 from account 0271112233 (Kofi Asante) was automatically blocked. Combined outflow of GHS 1,840 across 4 transfers in under 2 hours, all structured below the GHS 500 threshold to distinct recipients.
fraudAlerts.recommendedAction: Freeze wallet and block all outgoing transactions. Open fraud case for review. Notify compliance team.

fraudAlerts.alertMessage: GHS 8,500 deposited into account 0551987654 (Efua Ansah) at 02:15 AM at Accra - Madina Zongo Junction. Large deposits during midnight-to-5 AM window have a 94% correlation with synthetic deposit fraud patterns in Ghanaian MoMo data.
fraudAlerts.recommendedAction: Hold funds for 24 hours, request source-of-funds documentation, verify with agent before releasing.

fraudAlerts.alertMessage: Account 0551987654 (Efua Ansah) attempted to transfer out GHS 8,000 only 23 minutes after a flagged GHS 8,500 deposit, both at 2 AM. Near-total drain pattern detected. Transaction blocked automatically.
fraudAlerts.recommendedAction: Block all outgoing transactions. Escalate to fraud investigation team. Flag account for compliance review.

fraudAlerts.alertMessage: Account 0551987654 (Efua Ansah) attempted a GHS 400 cashout at Madina Market 27 minutes after a blocked transfer. Second outgoing attempt within 50 minutes of the original suspicious deposit. Coordinated drain behavior confirmed.
fraudAlerts.recommendedAction: Keep wallet frozen. File preliminary Suspicious Transaction Report (STR) with FIC. Initiate in-person account review.

fraudAlerts.alertMessage: Account 0271112233 (Kofi Asante) sent a second transfer of GHS 480 within 27 minutes of the first (GHS 450). Both amounts cluster just below the GHS 500 auto-review threshold. Early structuring signal detected.
fraudAlerts.recommendedAction: Flag for monitoring. If a third transfer occurs within 1 hour, escalate to fraud alert.

fraudCases.fraudPattern: Suspicious Large Deposit with Rapid Cash-Out Attempt
fraudCases.explanation: On 12 March 2026, account 0207654321 received a GHS 15,000 deposit at Kumasi - Kejetia Market using a previously unseen device and agent. The amount is 7,400% above the account's 30-day average deposit of GHS 200. Within 26 minutes, the holder attempted to withdraw GHS 14,500 at a different agent (Adum), which was automatically blocked. The prior transaction history shows only modest transfers (e.g. GHS 150). This pattern is consistent with money mule activity or deposit-and-flee fraud.
fraudCases.recommendedAction: Maintain 48-hour fund hold. Request in-person identity verification and source-of-funds documentation. If documentation is not provided within 72 hours, file a Suspicious Transaction Report (STR) with the Financial Intelligence Centre (FIC).

fraudCases.explanation: On 13 March 2026, account 0271112233 initiated 4 outgoing transfers within 99 minutes from Takoradi. The amounts — GHS 450, 480, 420, and 490 — all cluster just below the GHS 500 automatic review threshold, totalling GHS 1,840 to distinct recipients. The fourth transfer was automatically blocked by the AI fraud engine. This pattern is characteristic of transaction structuring (smurfing), a common technique used to layer illicit funds below detection thresholds.
fraudCases.recommendedAction: Freeze wallet and block all outgoing transfers. Investigate recipient accounts for connections. Contact account holder for explanation. If structuring is confirmed, file STR with the Financial Intelligence Centre (FIC) and consider permanent account restriction.

fraudCases.fraudPattern: Synthetic Deposit with Off-Hours Drain Attempt
fraudCases.explanation: On 14 March 2026, account 0551987654 received a GHS 8,500 deposit at 02:15 AM at Accra - Madina Zongo Junction. Within 23 minutes, a GHS 8,000 transfer_out was attempted and blocked. A second outgoing attempt (GHS 400 cashout at Madina Market) was blocked 27 minutes later. All activity occurred between 2-3 AM with no prior account history. The deposit-then-drain pattern at off-hours, using near-total withdrawal amounts across multiple channels, is consistent with synthetic deposit fraud or SIM-swap-enabled account takeover.
fraudCases.recommendedAction: Freeze wallet permanently pending investigation. File Suspicious Transaction Report (STR) with FIC. Request in-person identity re-verification. Investigate whether SIM swap was performed on phone number 0551987654. Coordinate with telecom provider for SIM activity logs.
